title: ficus infectoria var. aegeirophylla
intro: Ficus infectoria var. aegeirophylla, also known as the Indian fig tree, is a plant species native to India and Southeast Asia, characterized by its broad leaves and medicinal properties. The plant contains various compounds such as gallic acid and ellagic acid, which have been found to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Ficus infectoria var. aegeirophylla include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and protect against oxidative stress. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, to promote overall well-being and combat various health conditions.
